title: Spuriopimpinella calycina

This plant is further grouped within the subfamily Apioideae. Pimpinella species are often referred to as bishop's weed. Spuriopimpinella calycina is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia.

intro: Spuriopimpinella calycina is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), commonly found in mountainous regions. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Spuriopimpinella calycina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Spuriopimpinella calycina is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory infections and other inflammatory conditions.
